free_text: Absolute thick-target gamma-ray yields for elemental
Most of the targets were 1 mm thick, 1*1 cm**2 metallic
plates. In addition to the targets available in
elemental form, powdered chemical compound target were
used.  Only these are listed in Subentries.  The
samples were 1 mm thick pellets, 6 mm in diameter.
The proton beam current was adjusted to keep the
gamma-count rate constant and the dead time correction
below 1%.  It varied from 0.1 to 20 nano-Ampere
. A shielded 80 cm**3 Ge(Li) gamma-ray detector.
The Ge-Li detector was shielded from neutrons by a 10cm
thick paraffin absorber containing a small amount of
boron added to capture the thermalized neutrons.
In order to minimize possible angular distribution
effects on the gamma-ray yields, the detector was
located at an angle of 55 degrees relative to the beam.
. Neutrons were detected by a B-F3 counter located
The experimental uncertainties in the absolute
gamma-ray yield values are expected to be below 10%.
- By compiler. Some gamma quanta are absent in ENSDF.
Structure of the entry is changed too.
